title: grewia piscatorum
intro: Grewia piscatorum, also known as the fish poison tree or fish poison bush, is a plant species native to tropical Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The extracts from this plant are said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating pain, as well as showing potential in treating various diseases including malaria and tuberculosis. The herbal preparations of Grewia piscatorum typically involve boiling the leaves or roots in water to create a tea or infusion that can be used to treat various health conditions.
